title: Stores Designed Gift Cards to Steal $3 Billion From You
hook: There's probably $175 in gift cards buried in your home right now — and retailers deliberately built every obstacle to make sure you never spend it.
angle: Gift card 'breakage' — retailers book unredeemed card balances as pure profit, and every friction point (expiration fees, merchant limits, awkward checkout) is an intentional extraction mechanism, not an inconvenience
audience: Anyone who has received or given a gift card and forgotten about it — which is most adults in America
key_numbers: $21 billion in unspent gift card value currently held by Americans
$175 average unspent gift card value per household
$3 billion expires unused annually, booked as retailer 'breakage' profit
